Tracheal secretions should generally be suctioned within a short time frame to minimize exposure to harmful effects associated with longer durations of suctioning. Studies indicate that the optimal length for one suction episode ranges between 5 to 15 seconds under routine circumstances, with repeated cycles interspersed with periods allowing the patient's airway to re-oxygenate. Therefore, considering the standard protocols discussed in several referenced articles,
\boxed{\text{A}}
```plaintext
\boxed{A}
```
This aligns with current best practices aimed at reducing tissue damage and preserving overall patient stability. Longer suction times carry higher risks of inducing hypoxemia, irritation, and other adverse reactions. Thus, choosing option \( \text{A} \): "10-15 seconds" reflects modern guidelines recommending limited-duration suction episodes.
